head: 44c1476
summary: PR adds substantial structural test coverage for matcher validation, YAML round-trips, and the convertLegacyMatchers conversion (configured-input → produced-matcher), but the suite has weak behavioral coverage of the runtime matcher path: the new test that names matchers (TestFailsafeMatcherFinality) is a vacuous struct-field tautology, getFailsafeExecutor's new matcher branch has no end-to-end behavioral test at network/upstream level, the data/failsafe.go cache failsafe selector still ignores Matchers entirely (silent partial migration with zero tests), and CachePolicyConfig.convertLegacyMatchers silently drops finality-only and empty-only legacy configs (Bugbot-flagged) with no test exercising those inputs. The author-acknowledged 17 skipped subtests in TestSetDefaults_NetworkConfig_FailsafeMatchMethod have only one partial replacement assertion and no equivalent network-level coverage. No micro-benchmarks exist for the new matchers.MatchConfig hot path (Aram review #18 requested 8-10k rps benchmarks). Net assessment: tests prove configuration plumbing works; tests do NOT prove that production traffic gets routed to the right failsafe executor or cached under the right policy when configs use the new matchers field.

findings.title: TestFailsafeMatcherFinality is a vacuous test that asserts struct field assignment, not matcher behavior
findings.details: The test constructs a *common.FailsafeConfig{Matchers: [...], Retry: ...} literal and then asserts the field values match what was just set: assert.Len(cfg.Matchers, 1), assert.Equal("eth_getBlockByNumber", cfg.Matchers[0].Method), assert.Equal(DataFinalityStateFinalized, cfg.Matchers[0].Finality[0]). It never invokes matchers.MatchConfig, matchers.Match, getFailsafeExecutor, ExecuteRetryPolicy, or any other code path that would actually use the matcher to make a routing decision. It does not construct a NormalizedRequest, does not exercise SetDefaults/Validate, and does not verify that a request with eth_getBlockByNumber+Finalized finality actually selects this executor over a non-matching one. The test name ("TestFailsafeMatcherFinality") implies coverage of the new failsafe matcher finality behavior; the body covers only Go's struct-literal assignment.
findings.recommended_fix: Replace with a test that (a) creates 2+ FailsafeConfigs with different Matchers, (b) wires them through SetDefaults+Validate, (c) calls upstream.getFailsafeExecutor or network.getFailsafeExecutor with NormalizedRequests of varying method/finality, and (d) asserts the correct executor is returned. A minimum of 4 cases: matcher matches, matcher doesn't match (falls to next), finality matches but method doesn't, both must match (intersection).

findings.title: Missing end-to-end behavioral coverage for getFailsafeExecutor's new matcher branch
findings.details: Both upstream/upstream.go::getFailsafeExecutor and erpc/networks.go::getFailsafeExecutor were modified in this PR to prefer matcher-based selection when fe.config.Matchers is non-empty (upstream.go:297-302; networks.go:259-272). All existing TestGetFailsafeExecutor_* subtests in networks_failsafe_test.go (FirstMatchingPolicy_ByFinality_BeforeMethodOnlyMatch, SecondPolicy_WhenFirstDoesNotMatchFinality, GenericFallback_WhenNoSpecificMatch, WildcardMethodMatch_AnyMethod, WildcardPatternMatch_trace_star, PipeDelimitedMethodMatch, MultipleFinalitiesMatch — 17 subtests) construct configs with the LEGACY MatchMethod / MatchFinality fields and the test helper setupTestNetworkWithMultipleFailsafePolicies skips SetDefaults on the failsafe slice. The legacy fallback branch (networks.go:274-283 / upstream.go:304-322) is therefore the only branch these tests exercise. The new branch — Matchers populated, matchers.Match selects — has zero behavioral test asserting which executor is returned for a given request. There is also no test for the mixed case (some configs with Matchers, others legacy) or for matcher fields not expressible in legacy (Network, Params).
findings.recommended_fix: Add TestGetFailsafeExecutor_MatcherSelection covering: (a) single config with Matchers selects on matching request and is skipped on non-matching, (b) two configs with non-overlapping Matchers, request routes to correct one, (c) Network field on matcher (legacy can't express), (d) Params field on matcher (legacy can't express), (e) mixed legacy + matcher configs in the same Failsafe array, (f) all-matcher list with no fallback — verify behavior when nothing matches.

findings.title: convertLegacyMatchers silently drops finality-only and empty-only legacy CachePolicyConfig — no test exercises these inputs
findings.details: CachePolicyConfig.convertLegacyMatchers gates conversion on hasLegacyFields := c.Network != "" || c.Method != "" || len(c.Params) > 0. It does NOT include c.Finality != DataFinalityStateFinalized (the zero value) or c.Empty != CacheEmptyBehaviorIgnore (the zero value). When a user writes a policy with only `finality: unfinalized` or only `empty: allow` in YAML, hasLegacyFields evaluates to false, the legacy → matcher block is skipped, and the function falls through to the default catch-all `{Method: "*", Action: Include}` — silently discarding the user's finality and empty settings. The resulting policy will match ANY request rather than the intended subset. Bugbot flagged this. Test coverage: TestCachePolicySetDefaults has 5 subtests (config_test.go:1382-1469); the closest case `partial legacy fields are converted correctly` sets Network+Method but not finality/empty. There is NO test of (a) only Finality set, (b) only Empty set, (c) Finality + Empty set without Network/Method/Params. With Finality's zero value being DataFinalityStateFinalized, a user-set value of Unfinalized/Realtime/Unknown would silently disappear.
findings.recommended_fix: Add three subtests under TestCachePolicySetDefaults: (a) `only finality is set`: input {Finality: DataFinalityStateUnfinalized}, assert resulting matcher has Finality=[Unfinalized] (not catch-all); (b) `only empty is set`: input {Empty: CacheEmptyBehaviorAllow}, assert resulting matcher has Empty=Allow; (c) `finality and empty without network/method/params`: assert both propagate. These tests will fail today, surfacing the bug. Fix the gate to also include c.Finality != 0 || c.Empty != 0.

findings.title: data/failsafe.go::buildExecutors ignores Matchers — silent partial migration, no test catches it
findings.details: Cache failsafe (FailsafeConnector) builds CacheFailsafeExecutor entries by reading ONLY fsCfg.MatchMethod and fsCfg.MatchFinality (lines 76-83). It never inspects fsCfg.Matchers. After PR 388, a user can write a cache failsafe config with the new matchers: [...] schema (and empty MatchMethod/MatchFinality), expecting selection to honor network/method/params/finality/action constraints. buildExecutors will produce an executor with method="*" (default fallback) and finalities=nil — i.e. every cache operation matches this executor regardless of the user's matcher constraints. The matcher's Network, Params, Empty, Action fields are silently ignored. Tests in data/failsafe_test.go (TestCacheFailsafe_ExecutorSelection_ByMethodFromContext and others) all use legacy MatchMethod; there is no test that sets fsCfg.Matchers and asserts buildExecutors honors them. The PR is described as 'unified MatcherConfig system'; this connector path was not updated.
findings.recommended_fix: Either (1) update buildExecutors to call fsCfg.Normalize()/SetDefaults and use fsCfg.Matchers (via matchers.Match in getFailsafeExecutor for connectors), then add tests verifying matcher-based selection for cache failsafe; OR (2) explicitly document that cache-level failsafe does not support matchers and add a Validate() error when Matchers is set on a connector failsafe (with a test for the rejection). Either way, current state is 'silent half-migration' with no test.

findings.title: MatchesForGet cross-finality match (Finalized request → Unfinalized policy) untested
findings.details: MatchesForGetWithMatcher contains GET-specific finality semantics that diverge from matchers.MatchConfig: when finality == DataFinalityStateFinalized, the matcher.Finality slice is allowed to contain EITHER Finalized OR Unfinalized (rationale per code comment: 'data might have been cached as unfinalized but is now finalized'). The single test for this code path — `MatchesForGet with special finality handling` (cache_policy_test.go:94-121) — asserts only three cases: (1) exact finality match (Finalized→Finalized) → true, (2) Unknown finality → true, (3) Realtime → false. It does NOT exercise the cross-finality branch where matcher has Finality=[Unfinalized] but the request is Finalized. A regression here (e.g. someone removing the Unfinalized clause) would silently produce cache misses for finalized lookups whose data was cached during the unfinalized window — or worse, hits across finality classes that should not be conflated.
findings.recommended_fix: Add subtest: matcher with Finality=[Unfinalized], queried with finality=Finalized → expect true (with assertion on the returned matcher). Mirror it: matcher with Finality=[Finalized], queried with finality=Unfinalized → expect false. Also add: matcher with Finality=[Unfinalized] queried with finality=Realtime → expect false. These three pin down the asymmetric Finalized↔Unfinalized rule.

findings.title: TestSetDefaults_NetworkConfig_FailsafeMatchMethod entirely t.Skip'd (17 subtests, 600+ lines) — replacement coverage is partial
findings.details: TestSetDefaults_NetworkConfig_FailsafeMatchMethod was skipped in its entirety (line 529: t.Skip("Behavior removed by PR #388 (matchers); see review comments #11/#12")). The author's own comment in the test file (lines 522-528) states "they should be replaced with new coverage of the matchers-based defaulting logic before the PR merges" — and that has NOT happened. The 17 skipped subtests covered: UserFailsafeWithSpecificMethodNotOverwrittenByUnmatchedDefault, UserFailsafeWithMultipleSpecificMethodsPreserved, UserFailsafeReplacesDefaultsEvenWhenMethodAndFinalityMatch, UserFailsafeNoMethodDefaultHasMethod_NoMatch, UserFailsafeHasMethodDefaultNoMethod_NoMatch, BothNoMethod_ShouldMatch, FinalityMatchOnly_EmptyFinalityMatchesAny, FinalityMismatch_ShouldNotMatch, WildcardMethodMatch, PipePatternDoesNotMatchLiteralPipeValue, WildcardStarMatchesPipeValue, MultipleDefaultsFirstMatchWins, NoUserFailsafe_DefaultsCopied, EmptyDefaultsFailsafe_UserConfigPreserved, NoDefaults_UserConfigPreserved, PipePatternInDefaultMatchesSingleMethodValue, UnrelatedMethodDoesNotMatch. Replacement coverage in the diff: only TestSetDefaults_UpstreamConfig::UpstreamFailsafeUserPoliciesReplaceDefaults (defaults_test.go:282-320) covers the new "user policies stand alone" semantic, and only at upstream level. There is NO equivalent at network level, and zero tests for: (a) the NoUserFailsafe-defaults-copied path under matchers, (b) per-policy SetDefaults still applies system defaults (retry MaxAttempts, BackoffFactor, etc.) when matchers are present, (c) SetDefaults idempotence on Matchers, (d) the new behavior across the four NetworkDefaults×UserFailsafe combinations the prior tests covered.
findings.recommended_fix: Author should write a new TestSetDefaults_NetworkConfig_Matchers covering at minimum: (1) user has matchers + defaults has matchers → user matchers stand alone, (2) user has no failsafe + defaults has failsafe → defaults are deep-copied including Matchers, (3) user has matchers + no defaults → system defaults applied per-policy, (4) idempotence: calling SetDefaults twice produces the same Matchers, (5) UpstreamConfig and NetworkConfig parity (currently only Upstream has this case).

findings.title: No micro-benchmarks for matchers.MatchConfig / matchers.Match hot path — Aram review #18 unaddressed
findings.details: matchers.MatchConfig and matchers.Match are invoked on every request via getFailsafeExecutor (network and upstream) and on every cache lookup via MatchesForSetWithMatcher / MatchesForGetWithMatcher. Both functions allocate (string conversions in paramToString, slice iteration with len(matcher.Params) recursive matchParam calls). At sustained 8-10k rps cited in Aram review #18, the matcher cost compounds. No Benchmark* function exists in matchers/, and existing benchmarks in erpc/networks_bench_test.go (BenchmarkNetworkForward_*) are full-stack and don't isolate matcher cost. Without a baseline, any future regression in matchers/ can only be detected via end-to-end perf tests.
findings.recommended_fix: Add matchers/matcher_bench_test.go with: (a) BenchmarkMatchConfig_NetworkAndMethod (hot path, simple wildcard), (b) BenchmarkMatchConfig_DeepParams (object pattern matching), (c) BenchmarkMatch_TenConfigs (iteration cost), and a -benchmem variant. Capture baseline ns/op + B/op + allocs/op. This addresses Aram review #18 and gives future contributors a regression detector.

findings.title: matchers.Match precedence (last-takes-precedence) untested at failsafe level
findings.details: matchers.Match iterates configs from index len-1 down to 0 (line 176-181), returning the first match's Action. Cache policies exercise this via MatchesForSet/MatchesForGet ordering tests (cache_policy_test.go:43-68 for include+exclude; 181-237 for matched-matcher empty state). Failsafe selection through getFailsafeExecutor calls matchers.Match for the SINGLE FailsafeConfig's matchers; ordering between multiple matchers within one failsafe matters but is not tested at the failsafe level. Since validateMatchers prepends a catch-all (e.g. for include-only configs), the runtime ordering is non-trivial and a regression in Match's iteration direction would silently cause include-only matchers to fall through the catch-all exclude. Bugbot/Aram could trip on this.
findings.recommended_fix: Add a failsafe-level ordering test: FailsafeConfig with Matchers = [{Method:"*", Action:Exclude}, {Method:"eth_call", Action:Include}] (the post-validation shape for an include-only user config). Build via setupTestNetworkWithMultipleFailsafePolicies + cfg.SetDefaults+Validate, then call getFailsafeExecutor with eth_call request → verify it picks this fe; with eth_getBalance → verify it does NOT pick it (continues to next or returns last as default). This pins the iteration direction at the integration boundary.

findings.title: convertLegacyMatchers (FailsafeConfig) contains an unreachable branch — dead code masks a misunderstood contract
findings.details: The block `if len(f.Matchers) == 1 && f.Matchers[0].Method == "" && len(f.Matchers[0].Finality) == 0 { f.Matchers[0].Method = "*" }` (lines 2187-2189) is unreachable. We only enter the enclosing block when MatchMethod != "" || len(MatchFinality) > 0; if MatchMethod is non-empty, matcher.Method gets set to that non-empty value (line 2171); if MatchMethod is empty, MatchFinality must be non-empty, so matcher.Finality is non-empty. Both inner conditions of the if are mutually exclusive with the outer guard. No test can reach this branch — and the comment above it ("If we converted legacy fields and ended up with an empty method matcher AND no finality constraints") describes a state that cannot exist. The dead code is benign but suggests the author's mental model differs from the code's actual behavior; a future refactor could reintroduce a real bug here.
findings.recommended_fix: Delete the dead block. Add a test asserting that legacy MatchFinality=[Finalized] without MatchMethod produces a matcher with Method="" (not "*") and that subsequent Validate prepends the catch-all exclude — pinning the actual contract.

findings.title: Network field on MatcherConfig has weak behavioral coverage for failsafe path
findings.details: MatcherConfig.Network is a new field exclusive to the matcher schema (no legacy equivalent). matchers.MatchConfig (matcher.go:14-19) does WildcardMatch on networkId. cache_policy_test.go and config_test.go cover the Network field at the cache layer (TestCachePolicy_MatchersIntegration::MatchesForSet with include matchers checks Network="1" vs "2"). Failsafe-level tests do not exercise MatcherConfig.Network: getFailsafeExecutor extracts networkId via matchers.Match → req.NetworkId(), but no test sets a Network constraint on a failsafe matcher and verifies cross-network requests fall through. This is a primary new capability of the PR (per PR title: 'extend matchers to support params and finality in failsafe and upstream filtering').
findings.recommended_fix: Add subtest to TestGetFailsafeExecutor_*: configure a network or upstream with FailsafeConfig{Matchers: [{Network: "evm:1", Method: "*"}]} and a fallback. Issue requests with NetworkId() returning "evm:1" vs "evm:137"; verify the network-scoped matcher only matches the former.

residual_risks: Production deployments using the new matchers: schema for cache failsafe (FailsafeConnector / data/failsafe.go) will silently fall back to the wildcard "*" executor — user constraints on Network/Method/Params/Finality/Empty/Action are ignored. No test catches this; production load could expose it as wrong-policy execution (e.g. retry policy intended for read methods being applied to write methods, or vice versa).

Production deployments using YAML cache policies with only `finality:` or only `empty:` set will silently match all requests (Bugbot finding T-3). Risk: data caching at the wrong granularity, including potentially incorrect cross-finality cache hits.

All existing TestGetFailsafeExecutor_* coverage exercises the legacy fallback path. If a future refactor (intentionally or accidentally) populates fe.config.Matchers via SetDefaults at network-bootstrap time for ALL configs (including legacy ones), every existing test would silently switch to exercising the matcher path. The tests would still pass (because conversion is supposed to be lossless) but the legacy fallback in upstream.go:304-322 / networks.go:274-283 would lose its only test coverage. Recommend: (a) add explicit tests that pin the legacy path with `len(fe.config.Matchers) == 0` invariant, or (b) accept that the legacy path is dead and remove it.

matchers.Match is invoked on every request with no input validation (e.g. matcher.Method panics from regex compilation in WildcardMatch are not caught at config-load time uniformly). The validateMatchers function checks Action, but does NOT compile-test Method/Network wildcards. A malformed user wildcard like `[unclosed` could crash a hot path on first match attempt; no fuzz/property test covers this.

Aram review #18's request for 8-10k rps benchmarks is unfulfilled; no micro-benchmark for matchers.MatchConfig means there is no objective way to assess whether the new matcher path is fast enough for production load. The structural test coverage gives high confidence about correctness on the cases it tests, but zero confidence about performance at scale.

testing_gaps.area: End-to-end getFailsafeExecutor selection with the matcher path
testing_gaps.missing: Tests that construct a network/upstream with FailsafeConfig{Matchers: [...]} and verify which fe is returned for matching/non-matching requests at each of: (a) Method only, (b) Method+Finality, (c) Network, (d) Params, (e) Action=Exclude semantics, (f) mixed legacy + matcher configs in the same Failsafe array.

Either positive tests proving Matchers field flows through buildExecutors and getFailsafeExecutor selects correctly OR negative tests + Validate() error proving the cache layer rejects matcher configs. Currently zero coverage of either choice.
Inputs with only Finality set, only Empty set, both Finality+Empty without Network/Method/Params. Today's test suite cannot distinguish a working conversion from the silent-drop bug.
Cases with matcher Finality=[Unfinalized] queried with Finalized (should match) and the converse asymmetric variants — the most subtle behavior in the GET path.

No fuzz/property test for malformed wildcards in Method/Network. Validate() trusts user wildcards; runtime WildcardMatch may bubble errors that MatchConfig swallows (matcher.go:16,24,33 — `if err != nil || !match`). Errors are silently treated as non-matches, which means a typo'd wildcard becomes a permanently-non-matching policy with no signal.
matchers/matcher_bench_test.go with ns/op + B/op + allocs/op for MatchConfig/Match in representative shapes (single matcher, ten matchers, deep params). Aram review #18 explicitly requested this.

validateMatchers mutates its input slice (prepending catch-all). If Validate() is ever called more than once on the same FailsafeConfig (e.g. config reload, hot-reload), it will keep prepending. No test asserts validateMatchers idempotence: validateMatchers(validateMatchers(x)) == validateMatchers(x). Trivial bug surface.

17 subtests skipped; only the upstream-level UpstreamFailsafeUserPoliciesReplaceDefaults subtest covers the new no-merge semantics. Network-level equivalent of (BothNoMethod_ShouldMatch, FinalityMatchOnly_EmptyFinalityMatchesAny, NoUserFailsafe_DefaultsCopied, EmptyDefaultsFailsafe_UserConfigPreserved, MultipleDefaultsFirstMatchWins) for the matchers-based defaulting algorithm. Author's own comment acknowledges this gap.

summary_assessment.ship_readiness: NOT YET — three high-confidence high-severity findings (T-1 vacuous test, T-3 silent finality drop, T-4 cache failsafe ignores matchers) describe untested production-affecting bugs or false-confidence assertions. T-2 and T-6 describe core PR functionality that has no behavioral test asserting correctness. The PR is shippable from a 'compiles and existing tests pass' standpoint but the test suite does not provide CONFIDENCE that the new matcher behavior works correctly under the configurations users will actually write.
